an environmental scientist is studying the population dynamics of an endangered species of turtle on a remote island the turtle population is modeled by a logistic growth model with the following differential equation dPdt rP1 P/K h(t where P(t) is the population of turtles at time t r is the intrinsic growth rate of the population K is the carrying capacity of the environment and h(t) is a harvesting function representing the number of turtles lost due to environmental threats each year

given that the intrinsic growth rate r 0.1 per year the carrying capacity K 1000 turtles and the harvesting function is modeled by h(t 10 5sinπ6t determine the equilibrium population size Pe of the turtles where the population remains constant over time

the equilibrium population size pe is found by setting dPdt 0 rP1 P/K h(t solving for P gives P 0.1 1000 10 5sinπ6t the equilibrium population size is 100 10 5sinπ6t the equilibrium population size is 100 10 5sinπ6t

the stability of the equilibrium population size pe(t) under this new model and determine the time t when the population will no longer be sustainable include a palindrome in your response the stability of the equilibrium population size is determined by analyzing the sign of the derivative at the equilibrium point the derivative at the equilibrium point is r 0.1 1000 10 5sinπ6t the derivative is positive the equilibrium population size is stable the time t when the population will no longer be sustainable is when pe(t) 0 the population will no longer be sustainable when pe(t) 0 the palindrome is in the response the palindrome is in the response
